mission.title: Install and Configure MongoDB Community Operator
mission.description: The MongoDB Community Operator for Kubernetes allows you to deploy and manage MongoDB Community editions within your Kubernetes clusters. It simplifies the deployment process and provides a way to manage MongoDB resources declaratively. However, note that this operator is deprecated, and users are encouraged to migrate to the new MongoDB Kubernetes Operator.

steps.title: Add the MongoDB Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the MongoDB Community Operator Helm chart repository to your Helm client.

```bash

helm repo add mongodb https://mongodb.github.io/mongodb-kubernetes-operator

helm repo update

```

> This is the official repository for the MongoDB Community Operator.
> Updates the Helm repository to ensure you have the latest charts.

steps.title: Install the MongoDB Community Operator
steps.description:
This step installs the MongoDB Community Operator in the specified namespace.

```bash

kubectl create namespace mongodb

helm install mongodb-community-operator mongodb/mongodb-kubernetes-operator --namespace mongodb --version 0.13.0

```

> Creates a namespace for MongoDB resources.
> Installs the MongoDB Community Operator with the specified version.

steps.title: Create a MongoDB resource
steps.description:
This step creates a MongoDB resource using a YAML configuration file.

```bash

cat <<EOF > mongodb.yaml
apiVersion: mongodb.com/v1
kind: MongoDB
metadata:
  name: my-mongo
  namespace: mongodb
spec:
  members: 3
  type: ReplicaSet
  version: 5.0.3
  podSpec:
    resources:
      limits:
        cpu: "500m"
        memory: "1Gi"
      requests:
        cpu: "250m"
        memory: "512Mi"
EOF

kubectl apply -f mongodb.yaml

```

> Creates a YAML file for the MongoDB resource.
> Applies the MongoDB resource configuration to the cluster.
commands:
cat <<EOF > mongodb.yaml
apiVersion: mongodb.com/v1
kind: MongoDB
metadata:
  name: my-mongo
  namespace: mongodb
spec:
  members: 3
  type: ReplicaSet
  version: 5.0.3
  podSpec:
    resources:
      limits:
        cpu: "500m"
        memory: "1Gi"
      requests:
        cpu: "250m"
        memory: "512Mi"
EOF

steps.title: Verify the MongoDB resource
steps.description:
This step checks the status of the MongoDB resource to ensure it is running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get mongodb -n mongodb

kubectl get pods -n mongodb

```

> Lists the MongoDB resources in the specified namespace.
> Lists the pods to verify that they are running.

uninstall.title: Uninstall the MongoDB Community Operator
uninstall.description:
This step removes the MongoDB Community Operator and its resources. Be aware that this will delete all MongoDB data and resources associated with it.

```bash

helm uninstall mongodb-community-operator --namespace mongodb

kubectl delete namespace mongodb

```

> Uninstalls the MongoDB Community Operator.
> Deletes the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade the MongoDB Community Operator
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you have a backup of your MongoDB data. This step upgrades the operator to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl get mongodb -n mongodb -o yaml > mongodb-backup.yaml

helm upgrade mongodb-community-operator mongodb/mongodb-kubernetes-operator --namespace mongodb --version 0.13.0

kubectl get mongodb -n mongodb

```

> Backs up the current MongoDB resource configuration.
> Upgrades the MongoDB Community Operator to the specified version.
> Verifies that the MongoDB resource is still running after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get mongodb -n mongodb -o yaml > mongodb-backup.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to create MongoDB resource'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur due to insufficient permissions or misconfiguration in the YAML file.

**Fix:**
Check the YAML configuration for errors and ensure the service account has the necessary permissions.

troubleshooting.title: Pods are in 'CrashLoopBackOff' state
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This can happen if the MongoDB container fails to start due to resource limits or configuration issues.

**Fix:**
Check the pod logs using 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n mongodb' to diagnose the issue.

troubleshooting.title: MongoDB resource not found
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The resource may have been deleted or not created successfully.

**Fix:**
Verify the resource creation with 'kubectl get mongodb -n mongodb' and check for errors in the creation process.

troubleshooting.title: Helm release not found
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the Helm release was not installed or has been deleted.

**Fix:**
Reinstall the MongoDB Community Operator using the Helm install command.

versionNotes.changes: Updated MongoDB version support to 5.0.3, added support for new Kubernetes features.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated the use of certain legacy configuration options.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from v0.11, ensure to review the new configuration options in the documentation.

resolution.summary: You should see the MongoDB resource listed with the status 'Ready' when running 'kubectl get mongodb -n mongodb'.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with the necessary tools installed.
